decision_description: Phase 2 concept alignment assessment for MAESTRO against HORIZON-CL4-2026-05-DIGITAL-EMERGING-02. Confirmed: MAESTRO's three research pillars map to both expected outcomes — Pillar 1 (Neuro-Symbolic Planning and Reasoning) and Pillar 2 (Adaptive Memory Architecture) address EO-01 (significant improvements in autonomy, robustness, and reliability of AI agents through advanced planning mechanisms, memory management, and reasoning capabilities); Pillar 3 (Decentralised Multi-Agent Coordination) addresses EO-02 (innovative multi-agent frameworks and protocols demonstrating effective decentralised coordination beyond individual agent capabilities). Both mappings carry status Confirmed in concept_refinement_summary.json with direct evidence chains from Tier 3 concept_note.md, project_summary.json, and strategic_positioning.md. Strategic differentiation is grounded in the integrated co-design of all three pillars, validated across healthcare and advanced manufacturing demonstrators targeting TRL 5 with sector-leading industry partners. Unresolved: Three mandatory scope requirements remain unresolved and block the Phase 2 gate condition. SC-01 (against SR-04): external tool and API integration is designated an essential required element in Tier 2B scope_requirements.json but is absent from the concept as an explicit research pillar or named architectural commitment; implicit tool references in demonstrators do not satisfy the mandatory element. SC-02 (against SR-07 and CC-10): MAESTRO's third demonstrator targets autonomous logistics and supply chain planning; the call's three specifically named Apply AI sectors are healthcare, advanced manufacturing, and in-vehicle autonomous driving; logistics is not listed as an Apply AI Strategy priority sector in any Tier 2B source; the strategic positioning document's claim that logistics is an Apply AI Strategy priority sector is factually inconsistent with Tier 2B and must be corrected. SC-03 (against SR-06): the concept describes validation through demonstrators but contains no explicit KPI framework, benchmarking methodology, or structured progress monitoring plan; the mandatory SR-06 element is not satisfied at concept stage. Phase 2 gate condition requires that no unresolved scope conflicts remain between the concept and Tier 2B; this condition is not met. Concept alignment is partially achieved: expected outcome coverage is confirmed; mandatory scope compliance has three open blocking gaps requiring human operator resolution before Phase 3 may commence.
alternatives_considered: Proceed to Phase 3 treating SC-01, SC-02, and SC-03 as non-blocking advisory findings — rejected: SR-04, SR-06, and SR-07 are each designated mandatory (mandatory: true) in Tier 2B scope_requirements.json; treating mandatory scope requirements as advisory is a constitutional violation of CLAUDE.md §12.4 and §15; the system must prefer explicit gate failure over fabricated completion

Record concept alignment as fully resolved by inferring tool and API integration from demonstrator descriptions (clinical data gathering, manufacturing process monitoring) — rejected: CLAUDE.md §10.5 requires all material claims to be traceable to tiered inputs; implicit tool use in demonstrators does not constitute an explicit research commitment to SR-04's mandatory element; CLAUDE.md §13.3 prohibits inventing project facts not present in Tier 3

Record SC-02 as resolved by treating MAESTRO's two confirmed Apply AI sector demonstrators (healthcare and advanced manufacturing) as sufficient to satisfy SR-07's minimum — partially accepted as a mitigation framing but not as full resolution: the CC-10 portfolio logic explicitly prioritises in-vehicle autonomous driving as the third named sector for portfolio slot allocation; the strategic_positioning.md framing error (claiming logistics as an Apply AI Strategy priority sector) remains a Tier 2B consistency violation that must be corrected in Tier 3 documents before this conflict can be closed

Replace the logistics demonstrator with an in-vehicle autonomous driving demonstrator to resolve SC-02 — considered but not decided autonomously: this would require modification of Tier 3 project data (project_summary.json, concept_note.md, strategic_positioning.md) and consortium capability re-evaluation; per CLAUDE.md §3 authority hierarchy, changes to Tier 3 project instantiation data require explicit human operator instruction
tier_authority_applied: Tier 2B scope_requirements.json (docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/scope_requirements.json) — SR-04, SR-06, SR-07 mandatory requirements; Tier 2B expected_outcomes.json (docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/expected_outcomes.json) — EO-01, EO-02 mapping confirmation; Tier 2B call_constraints.json (docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/call_constraints.json) — CC-10 portfolio coverage logic; CLAUDE.md §7 phase_02_concept_refinement gate condition — requires no unresolved scope conflicts between concept and Tier 2B before gate passes
rationale: EO-01 and EO-02 mappings are confirmed with direct evidence traceable from Tier 3 to Tier 2B per the concept_refinement_summary.json topic_mapping_rationale, satisfying the vocabulary alignment and strategic differentiation sub-conditions of the Phase 2 gate. However, the gate condition in CLAUDE.md §7 for phase_02_concept_refinement explicitly requires that 'no unresolved scope conflicts remain between the concept and Tier 2B'; the concept_refinement_summary.json scope_conflict_log records three conflicts (SC-01, SC-02, SC-03) each with resolution_status 'unresolved'. All three conflicts reference mandatory Tier 2B requirements (SR-04 mandatory:true, SR-06 mandatory:true, SR-07 mandatory:true). Resolving these conflicts requires either modification of Tier 3 project data or an explicit human operator decision to accept the gap with documented justification — neither of which has occurred. This decision is recorded to constitute a durable, reproducible record of the Phase 2 alignment outcome per CLAUDE.md §9.4, to satisfy the requirement that every decision affecting future interpretation be written to the decision log rather than held in agent memory, and to provide a traceable basis for gate condition evaluation by the scheduler.
